---
title: "prepocessing"
output: html_document
date: "2026-07-19"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

---
# ---- Load packages ----------------------------------------------------------
---
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)     # tidy up messy column names
```


```{r}
# ---- Load Dataset ----------------------------------------------------------
data_path = "C:/Users/farza/Job/Github/Melbourne-Housing-Market/data/raw/Melbourne_housing_FULL.csv"
df = read.csv(data_path)
```

```{r}
# ---- summary of dataset
summary(df)
```

```{r}
# ---- Analysing null values
colSums(is.na(df))
```

```{r}
# ---- Step 1: Standardise column names ---------------------------------------
# clean_names() converts every column name to lower_snake_case and removes
# spaces / capitals. It does NOT fix spelling, so we rename the misspelled
# and awkward columns ourselves with rename() -> new_name = old_name.
df <- df |>
  clean_names() |>
  rename(
    latitude       = lattitude,     # fix source misspelling
    longitude      = longtitude,    # fix source misspelling
    region_name    = regionname,    # add the missing underscore
    property_count = propertycount
  )
```

```{r}
# ---- Check the result -------------------------------------------------------
names(df)   # see the cleaned column names
```

```{r}
# ---- Step 2: Convert the date column to a real date type --------------------

# First, count missing dates BEFORE conversion.
na_before <- sum(is.na(df$date))

# Convert text (day/month/year) to a real Date.
housing <- df |>
  mutate(date = dmy(date))

# Count missing dates AFTER conversion.
na_after <- sum(is.na(df$date))

# ---- Verify the conversion was clean ----------------------------------------
na_before                                   # missing before
na_after                                    # missing after (should be equal)
range(df$date, na.rm = TRUE)           # sensible date range?
range(month(df$date), na.rm = TRUE)    # should be 1 to 12

```


```{r}
# ---- Step 3: Remove rows with a missing price -------------------------------
# price is our target variable, so rows without it cannot be used and should
# not be imputed (see README, "Data Cleaning Decisions"). We drop them.

# Count rows before, so we can see how many we remove.
rows_before <- nrow(housing)

housing <- housing |>
  filter(!is.na(price))

# Count rows after.
rows_after <- nrow(housing)

# ---- Check the result -------------------------------------------------------
rows_before                    # rows before dropping
rows_after                     # rows after dropping
rows_before - rows_after       # how many rows were removed
sum(is.na(housing$price))      # should now be 0
```
```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```
